title: Fix story feed distance filtering to use story's saved coordinates

requirements.text: Ensure every story is saved with its own latitude and longitude fields (story.latitude and story.longitude) captured at the time of story creation — these must reflect the story's pinned location, not the uploader's current device location.

quotes: Each story must be saved with: story.latitude, story.longitude

acceptanceCriteria: The Story type in backend/main.mo includes latitude and longitude fields.
When a story is created, the latitude and longitude stored are those provided by the creator (the picked/pinned map location), not derived from any user profile location.
Existing stories without explicit coordinates do not cause runtime errors (graceful fallback).

requirements.text: Fix the frontend feed distance filtering logic so that the base coordinates for filtering are determined as follows: if a teleportedLocation exists, use its lat/lng as (baseLat, baseLng); otherwise use the user's current geolocation as (baseLat, baseLng). Then compare (baseLat, baseLng) against each story's own (story.latitude, story.longitude) using the Haversine distance. Stories whose distance from the base exceeds the selected distance range must be excluded from the feed. The uploader's location must never be used for this comparison.

quotes: If teleportedLocation exists → filter stories within selected distance from teleportedLocation
Else → filter stories within selected distance from user's current geolocation
Distance filtering must compare: (baseLat, baseLng) with (story.latitude, story.longitude)
Do NOT use uploader location for filtering.
If story is outside selected distance range → do not show in feed.

acceptanceCriteria: In HomePage.tsx (and any other feed view), the distance filter reads story.latitude and story.longitude from each story object as the story coordinates.
When teleportedLocation is set, baseLat/baseLng is sourced from teleportedLocation.
When teleportedLocation is not set, baseLat/baseLng is sourced from the user's current geolocation.
The uploader's profile location or any user-profile-derived coordinates are never used in the distance comparison.
Stories beyond the selected distance slider value are not rendered in the feed.
The locationFilter utility in frontend/src/lib/locationFilter.ts (or wherever distance filtering is implemented) compares against story.latitude and story.longitude exclusively.

requirements.text: Update the CreateStoryDialog so that when a story is submitted, the latitude and longitude sent to the backend are taken from the map location picker (the pinned story location), not from the user's current device geolocation.

quotes: Each story must be saved with: story.latitude, story.longitude
Do NOT use uploader location for filtering.

acceptanceCriteria: CreateStoryDialog.tsx passes the map-picker coordinates as the story's latitude and longitude when calling the create/publish backend function.
If no location is picked on the map, the story cannot be submitted (or a sensible fallback is applied and documented).
The user's live device geolocation is not used as the story's latitude/longitude.

constraints: Do not change uploader profile data or any user profile location fields.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko changes must remain within the single actor in backend/main.mo.
nonGoals: Changing the UI appearance of story cards or the feed layout.
Adding new filtering dimensions beyond distance.
Modifying the teleportedLocation feature itself — only consume it correctly in the filter.
